title: Chinnamastā Sādhana
description: The third of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Chinnamastā ("she of the severed head") is iconographically depicted as self-decapitated, holding her own severed head while three streams of blood feed her two attendants (Ḍākinī and Varṇinī) and her own mouth. High caution — an advanced Mahāvidyā rite whose iconography encodes a deliberate reversal of the polarity of life and death. Preserved here as heritage /
